records.ety: [L. vulnerabilis wounding, injurious, from vulnerare to wound, vulnus a wound; akin to Skr. vra<?/a: cf. F. vulnrable.]

defs: Capable of being wounded; susceptible of wounds or external injuries; as, a vulnerable body.
quotes: Achilles was vulnerable in his heel; and there will be wanting a Paris to infix the dart.

defs: Liable to injury; subject to be affected injuriously; assailable; as, a vulnerable reputation.
quotes: His skill in finding out the vulnerable parts of strong minds was consummate.
